Title: Temperature-dependent jumonji demethylase modulates flowering time by targeting H3K36me2/3 in Brassica rapa

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: Brassica rapa subsp. chinensis var. parachinensis / Brassica rapa subsp. chinensis
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: Par-derived allele BrJMJ18Par; PC/DG-prominent allele BrJMJ18PC
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Par inbred line 16A-1; DG HN129 (ZJHYQ); PC261 (ZYC); Par CX268 (SNLBLY70TCX)
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Chromatin modifier; Jumonji C-domain histone demethylase
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Jumonji family protein with JmjN, JmjC, Zf-C5HC2, FYRN, FYRC domains
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Epigenetic regulation of flowering-time control and heat-responsive growth; chlorophyll biosynthesis under high temperature
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Histone H3K36me2/3 demethylation at target loci; temperature-dependent chromatin binding/dissociation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: BrFLC3; BrFLC1; BrFLC2; chlorophyll/tetrapyrrole biosynthesis-related genes including HEME2; carbon metabolism-related genes including ATPD, IAR4, PGK1, SBPASE, EMB3105
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Under NC, overexpression represses BrFLC3 expression; under HT, BrJMJ18PC continues to repress BrFLC3 whereas BrJMJ18Par dissociates from BrFLC3, increasing H3K36me3 and elevating BrFLC3 expression in overexpression lines; BrJMJ18Par also downregulates several chlorophyll biosynthesis/heat-responsive genes under HT
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key temperature-dependent epigenetic switch controlling thermotolerant flowering and growth in Par; BrJMJ18Par uniquely delays flowering and improves biomass/quality under heat
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: High temperature (29/29°C greenhouse HT; extreme summer tunnel greenhouse heat); ambient/normal temperature
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Not established; authors speculate possible balancing/feedback with H3K27 demethylases
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BrFLC3 chromatin association; no stable protein partner experimentally validated
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: BrJMJ18 itself is an epigenetic regulator. It removes H3K36me2/3 globally and at BrFLC3-associated chromatin; high temperature reduces chromatin binding/activity more strongly for BrJMJ18Par than BrJMJ18PC
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: BrJMJ18 was the most highly expressed BrJMJ in the candidate region; expression detectable during floral transition
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in young rosette leaves during floral transition; differentially expressed during floral transition in DG and Par under NC; no major DG/Par expression difference in HT response reported
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter in Par overexpression lines; AtJMJ18 native promoter in Arabidopsis transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthesis and plant growth maintenance under heat
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: BrJMJ18Par enhances resilience under heat by delaying flowering and increasing aboveground biomass/leaf number/commercial quality under HT; BrJMJ18PC lacks this heat-adaptive effect. BrJMJ18 overexpression promotes flowering under NC, while BrJMJ18Par overexpression delays flowering under HT
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BrJMJ18 is the causal candidate in a thermotolerance QTL/selection sweep and encodes a nuclear H3K36me2/3 Jumonji demethylase. 2. BrJMJ18Par and BrJMJ18PC both promote flowering under normal conditions, but under high temperature BrJMJ18Par uniquely delays flowering and improves plant growth/quality. 3. BrJMJ18 directly associates with BrFLC loci, with BrJMJ18Par preferentially affecting BrFLC3 in a temperature-dependent manner. 4. Under heat, BrJMJ18Par dissociates from BrFLC3, elevating H3K36me3 and BrFLC3 expression in overexpression plants, consistent with delayed flowering. 5. Integrated ChIP-seq/RNA-seq indicates BrJMJ18Par also modulates chlorophyll biosynthesis and heat-responsive growth programs under high temperature. 6. Amino acid substitutions T345A, Y633C and L654F contribute to functional divergence between BrJMJ18Par and BrJMJ18PC
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL/BSA mapping; haplotype/selection sweep analysis; transgenic overexpression in B. rapa and Arabidopsis; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; in vitro demethylase assays with recombinant proteins; transient tobacco expression; ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; RNA-seq; qRT-PCR; field and greenhouse phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal conditions 22/22°C long day; high temperature 29/29°C long day; extreme summer tunnel greenhouse heat up to 52°C day
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Under NC, overexpression causes earlier bolting and slight/no biomass decrease; CRISPR knockout causes late flowering but does not improve biomass under HT, indicating growth benefit is not simply loss of function
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: BrJMJ18Par is a promising natural allele/germplasm for breeding heat-resilient B. rapa with minimal penalty under normal conditions; useful for allele introgression or editing/stacking strategies targeting temperature-resilient flowering and yield stability
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Whole-genome resequencing of 210 accessions; RNA-seq; ChIP-seq (NCBI GEO: GSE223969); raw sequencing data at GSA/CNCB: PRJCA025632
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Primary trait is flowering time/thermotolerance rather than classical nutrient content. Nutrient-metabolism relevance comes from regulation of chlorophyll biosynthesis and carbon-metabolism-associated genes under heat. Three amino acid changes (T345A, Y633C, L654F) contribute to allele-specific behavior. Applied species also included Arabidopsis thaliana and Nicotiana benthamiana for functional assays

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Par; DG; BrJMJ18 transgenic Par lines
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Developmental control of flowering with indirect consequences for heat resilience and growth
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of flowering; expression modulated by BrJMJ18-dependent H3K36me3 chromatin status
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key downstream regulator most strongly linked to BrJMJ18-mediated flowering response in Par
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: BrJMJ18 chromatin association at BrFLC3 locus
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: H3K36me3 at BrFLC3 locus is reduced when BrJMJ18 represses it under NC; under HT in BrJMJ18Par-OX plants, BrJMJ18Par dissociation increases H3K36me3 and BrFLC3 expression
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Dominant expressed BrFLC during flowering in Par; only downregulated BrFLC during floral transition in Par under field conditions
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in rosette leaves; decreased in BrJMJ18PC-OX and BrJMJ18Par-OX under NC; under HT decreased in BrJMJ18PC-OX but increased in BrJMJ18Par-OX
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher BrFLC3 expression under HT in BrJMJ18Par-OX lines is associated with delayed flowering
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BrFLC3 is the principal BrFLC linked to BrJMJ18Par-mediated thermoresponsive flowering. 2. BrJMJ18Par binds predominantly to BrFLC3, and heat abolishes this binding. 3. Increased H3K36me3 and expression of BrFLC3 under HT in BrJMJ18Par-OX lines correlate with delayed flowering. 4. In WT Par, BrFLC3 contribution is less direct/straightforward due to multilayer regulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; qRT-PCR; H3K36me3 ChIP-PCR; transgenic BrJMJ18 overexpression analyses
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream marker/pathway component for thermotolerant flowering, though authors emphasize its effect is less direct in WT Par
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: ChIP-seq and RNA-seq within study; GSE223969; PRJCA025632
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core downstream regulator because it is experimentally validated in Results as the main BrJMJ18 target mediating flowering response
